Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

What is the last verse of Gurvastakam

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2009-10-24 · 5 answers
We for some reason publish till the 8th verse, but all astakam have to have the 9th verse where the Phalam is presented, does anyone knows what is the 9th verse of Gurvastakam?
user [154] · 2009-10-24
Got it...

shrimad-guror ashtakam etad uccair
brahme muhurte pathati prayatnat
yas tena vrindavana-natha-sakshatsevaiva
labhya janusho '92nta eva (9)

One who during the brahma-muhurta hour carefully recites these eight prayers glorifying the spiritual master will at the end of this life attain direct service to the Lord of Vrndavana.

(anyone has it with diacritics ;-) ?
user [38] · 2009-10-24
ZrImad-guror aSTakam etad uccair
brAhme muhUrte paThati prayatnAt
yas tena vRndAvana-nAtha-sAkSAt
sevaiva labhyA juSaNo nta eva
user [154] · 2009-10-25
We have a problem: is it juSaNo or jaNuSo? Thanks!
user [38] · 2009-10-25
juSaNaH - engaging in, cultivating
jaNuSo - not a Sanskrit word (?)
user [154] · 2009-10-25
[quote][cite] VEDA:[/cite]juSaNaH - engaging in, cultivating
jaNuSo - not a Sanskrit word (?)[/quote]
Thank you, it is just that two sources were saying januso ( a strange word..) Great to have it resolved.
